user_message: Can you help me draft the follow-up? The customer (Fabrikam Residences) is asking about forecast prep.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Kevin Lee <kevin.lee@fabrikamresi.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-02-07 15:06 EST
Subject: URGENT: forecast prep Issue at Fabrikam Residences

Just checking in on the forecast prep proposal. Our VP Operations is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
To: Kevin Lee <kevin.lee@fabrikamresi.com>
Date: 2024-01-16 16:40 EST
Subject: Re: Re: forecast prep Timeline

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about forecast prep. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

Meeting: forecast prep Discussion - Fabrikam Residences
Date: 2024-02-04
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Kevin Lee (Fabrikam Residences), Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft), Priya (Fabrikam Residences)

[00:01:15] Kevin Lee (Fabrikam Residences): Appreciate everyone making time. The main agenda today is forecast prep.

[00:07:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): Our team lead reviewed this and has concerns about timeline constraints. Can we address those?

[00:12:00] Priya (Fabrikam Residences): The timeline for stakeholder alignment is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:15:00] Priya (Fabrikam Residences): Our CTO reviewed this and has concerns about stakeholder alignment. Can we address those?

[00:19:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is security compliance.

[00:24:00] Kevin Lee (Fabrikam Residences): On the forecast prep front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around timeline constraints.

[00:26:00] Priya (Fabrikam Residences): The timeline for technical validation is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:31:00] Priya (Fabrikam Residences): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is security compliance.

[00:33:00] Priya (Fabrikam Residences): On the forecast prep front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around data residency.

[00:36:00] Priya (Fabrikam Residences): The timeline for integration complexity is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

signals.detail: Signal detected for Fabrikam Residences: the cost projections need to factor in reserved instance pricing for accuracy

usability_anchors: Score 5: Complete, well-structured output matching the Stakeholder Map Generation template with all required artifacts present and actionable
Score 3: Output covers main requirements but missing one or two secondary artifacts or lacking specificity
Score 1: Output is generic, missing key artifacts, or fails to address the specific request
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct forecast prep decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
